steps.title: Identify the cause-and-effect relationship
steps.content: Betelgeuse became 40% brighter; its ranking rose from 10th to 7th. The key signal is that these two events are causally linked—the first produced the second.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is cause-effect: Betelgeuse became 40% brighter, and as a direct result its ranking rose from 10th to 7th. The blank needs a transition signaling that one event produced the other.
  • Choice A — in sum,: signals a concluding summary, so fails — the ranking change is a consequence, not a wrap-up of multiple prior points. ✗
  • Choice B — in turn,: signals a direct consequence or chain reaction, fitting perfectly — the brightness increase produced the ranking rise. ✓
  • Choice C — specifically,: signals a narrowing elaboration or detail, so fails — the ranking is a separate outcome, not a more precise restatement of the brightness fact. ✗
  • Choice D — for example,: signals an illustrative instance, so fails — the ranking change is a result of the brightness change, not an example of it. ✗
That is the correct answer.
